In the 2022 rugby league season, [[York City Knights]] competed in the [[2022 RFL Championship]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. At the start of the season the club was taken over by Clint Goodchild who became the new chairman. York had a strong first half of the season which included a run of seven wins in the league that helped them finish in sixth place to qualify for the play-offs despite winning only five of their final 11 games. They lost in the semi-finals to [[Leigh Centurions]] who earlier in the season inflicted a [[York Knights#Records|club record]] 100–4 defeat on York. In the Challenge Cup York reached the fifth round. It was the final season that the team played as York City Knights after which the club was renamed York RLFC and the team became the Knights.

==Season review==
===Pre-season===
In November 2021, [[James Ford (rugby league)|James Ford]] signed a new contract extending his contract as head coach until the end of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=James Ford commits to long-term deal at York City Knights |url=https://www.totalrl.com/james-ford-commits-to-long-term-deal-at-york-city-knights/ |website=Total Rugby League |date=7 November 2021 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> [[Brett Delaney]] was appointed as assistant coach, but left in July to join [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36257/york-city-knights-appoint-brett-delaney-as-assistant-coach |title=York City Knights appoint Brett Delaney as Assistant Coach |publisher=RFL |date=11 November 2021 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brett-delaney-leaves-york-for-hull-kr-super-league |title=Brett Delaney leaves York for Super League opportunity with Hull KR |website=Love Rugby League |date=6 July 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> Several players retired at the end of the 2021 season including [[Tim Spears]], who had been at York since the 2017 season,<ref name=Spears/> and the pre-season saw numerous player transfers followed by the departure of Jon Flatman as chairman when the club came under the new ownership of Clint Goodchild in January 2022.<ref name=review>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/23019616.york-city-knights-2022-season-review/ |title=The York City Knights 2022 season review |newspaper=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=4 October 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36449/york-city-knights-confirm-sale-of-club |title=York City Knights confirm sale of club |publisher=RFL |date=16 January 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref><ref name=InOut>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-ins-and-outs-for-2022-signings-and-retentions-club-by-club |title=Championship ins and outs for 2022: Signings and retentions club-by-club |website=Love Rugby League |date=17 November 2021 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref>

===2022 Season===
York began the season on 31 January with a Monday night fixture against [[Featherstone Rovers]] in which York lost 30–12.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-city-knights-praised-after-battling-featherstone-defeat |title=York City Knights praised after battling Featherstone defeat |website=Love Rugby League |date=31 January 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> In February, York won against [[Dewsbury Rams]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36567/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-round-two |title=Betfred Championship Round-Up {{!}} Round Two |publisher=RFL |date=6 February 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> and [[Batley Bulldogs]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36609/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-round-3 |title=Betfred Championship Round-Up {{!}} Round 3 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> before losing to [[Leigh Centurions]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36652/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-round-4 |title=Betfred Championship round-up {{!}} Round 4 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> [[Jack Logan (rugby league)|Jack Logan]], who had signed at the start of the season, announced his retirement from the game,<ref name=Logan/> and York defeated [[Newcastle Thunder]] 42–13 in the fourth round of the [[Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36686/betfred-challenge-cup-round-up-%7C-round-4 |title=Betfred Challenge Cup round-up {{!}} Round 4 |publisher=RFL |date=27 February 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> At the start of March, York moved to fifth in the league with a 26–24 win over [[London Broncos]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36817/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-6 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 6 |publisher=RFL |date=17 March 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> but were knocked out of the Challenge Cup by [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] a week later.<ref name=WhitehavenCup/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36786/betfred-challenge-cup-round-five-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round Five Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> However, the [[York Valkyrie|women's team]] continued their [[Women's Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] campaign with a double-header played with York's next league game; a 32–6 win over [[Widnes Vikings]] in which Levi Edwards made his debut for York and scored his first try for the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36811/our-league-to-show-york-double-header-this-sunday |title=Our League to show York double-header this Sunday! |publisher=RFL |date=16 March 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36834/betfred-championship-round-six-round-up |title=Betfred Championship Round Six Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=20 March 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> His second came on 3 April as York won 44–18 away to [[Workington Town]] in what was also the final game for [[Kristian Brining]] who announced his retirement a few weeks later.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/york-city-knights-and-former-salford-red-devils-star-announces-immediate-retirement-due-to-injury-52114/ |title=York City Knights and former Salford Red Devils star announces immediate retirement due to injury |website=Serious About Rugby League |date=21 April 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36951/betfred-championship-round-7-round-up |title=Betfred Championship Round 7 round-up |publisher=RFL |date=3 April 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> 

York recorded home wins over Newcastle<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37095/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-9 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 9 |publisher=RFL |date=22 April 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> and Whitehaven in April, and started May by defeating [[Barrow Raiders]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60017/betfred-championship-round-11-preview |title=Betfred Championship Round 11 Preview |publisher=RFL |date=12 May 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> and winning 20–6 at [[Bradford Bulls]]. The run of seven successive league wins, which had taken York up to third in the table, was ended the following week with a 40–24 loss to [[Halifax Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60071/betfred-championship-round-12-preview |title=Betfred Championship Round 12 Preview |publisher=RFL |date=19 May 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60099/betfred-championship-round-12-round-up |title=Betfred Championship Round 12 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=22 May 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> New signing [[Bailey Antrobus]] made his debut in the game and scored his first try for York the following week in a 34–14 win at [[Sheffield Eagles]].<ref name=Antrobus/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60187/betfred-championship-round-13-round-up |title=Betfred Championship Round 13 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=2 June 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> York continued to be successful throughout June with a closely fought 36–34 victory over London,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60199/betfred-championship-round-14-round-up |title=Betfred Championship Round 14 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=5 June 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> followed by away wins at Widnes and Whitehaven. The Whitehaven game was the first ever victory for the City Knights at the [[Recreation Ground (Whitehaven)|Recreation Ground]] and the first by a York team since 1992.<ref name=WhitehavenCup/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60352/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-round-16 |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Round 16 |publisher=RFL |date=23 June 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60377/betfred-championship-round-16-round-up |title=Betfred Championship Round 16 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=26 June 2022 |access-date=12 January 2024}}</ref> This run of four wins was followed by matches against Barrow, Halifax, Batley and Featherstone, all of whom finished above York in the league table. At the end of July, York defeated Newcastle at the Championship [[Summer Bash]] to end a run of four losses,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60611/betfred-championship-round-21-preview-%7C-summer-bash |title=Betfred Championship Round 21 Preview {{!}} Summer Bash |publisher=RFL |date=28 July 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60639/betfred-championship-round-22-round-up-%7C-summer-bash |title=Betfred Championship Round 22 Round-Up {{!}} Summer Bash |publisher=RFL |date=21 August 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> but three weeks later suffered a club record 100–4 loss at Leigh.<ref name=leigh100>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/20680487.york-city-knights-fall-record-100-4-defeat-leigh/ |title=York City Knights fall to record 100–4 defeat at Leigh |newspaper=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=21 August 2022|access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> A run of three wins at the end of the season, including a 74–12 victory over Workington meant that York finished their Championship campaign in sixth place.<ref name=review/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60897/betfred-championship-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Championship Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=11 September 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60930/betfred-championship-preview-%7C-play-off-eliminators- |title=Betfred Championship Preview {{!}} Play-Off Eliminators |publisher=RFL |date=15 September 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref>

In the play-offs, York won 26–24 at Halifax to reach the semi-finals,<ref name=FaxElim>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/22303721.york-city-knights-upset-halifax-26-24-play-off-victory/|title=York City Knights upset Halifax in 26–24 play-off victory|newspaper=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=10 January 2024}}</ref> where their season was ended by a 70–10 loss to Leigh.<ref name=leigh70>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/22639223.york-city-knights-play-off-run-ends-70-10-loss-leigh/ |title=York City Knights' play-off run ends in 70–10 loss at Leigh |newspaper=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=25 September 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref> At the Championship Awards York's [[AJ Towse]] was a nominee for the Young Player of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60916/betfred-championship-and-league-one-awards-nominations |title=Betfred Championship and League One Awards Nominations |publisher=RFL |date=12 September 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref>

===Post-season===
In October 2022, James Ford resigned from his role as head coach to become assistant coach at [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/23038732.james-ford-resigns-york-city-knights-head-coach/ |title=James Ford resigns as York City Knights head coach |work=The Press |date=11 October 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=James Ford resigns as York City Knights head coach after transformative reign |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/23038732.james-ford-resigns-york-city-knights-head-coach/ |access-date=10 January 2024 |work=The Press |date=11 October 2022}}</ref> On 14 October it was announced that the club had been renamed York RLFC and that the men's team would be rebranded as the Knights.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/riseup |title=#RiseUp |website=York RLFC |date=14 October 2022 |access-date=10 January 2024}}</ref>
